title: Extended tunneled direct link setup discovery method
abstract: Methods, systems, and apparatuses for wireless communication are described at a device. A device may utilize telescopic discovery techniques to efficiently preserve a wireless connection until a previously connected device returns within range of the connection. In one example, the device may transmit a discovery request intended for the other device according to a first interval after determining the wireless connection. If a response is not received, the device may adjust the transmission interval to be a second interval. The device may then compare the second interval with a maximum interval to determine whether the second interval is greater than the maximum interval. If the second interval is less than the maximum the device may transmit a discovery request according to the second interval. Otherwise, the device may transmit discovery requests according to the maximum interval until a discovery response is received from the other device.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of wireless communication at a first device, comprising: establishing a device-to-device wireless connection with a second device; transmitting a first discovery request at a first transmission interval; determining whether a discovery response to the first discovery request is received from the second device; and adjusting the first transmission interval to a second transmission interval based at least in part on the determination. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the device-to-device wireless connection comprises a tunneled direct link setup (TDLS) connection. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: comparing the second transmission interval with a maximum transmission interval. 4. The method of claim 3, further comprising: determining the second transmission interval is less than the maximum transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison; and transmitting a second discovery request at the second transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison. 5. The method of claim 3, further comprising: determining the second transmission interval is greater than the maximum transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison; and transmitting a second discovery request at the maximum transmission interval. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the second transmission interval is a multiple of the first transmission interval. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein adjusting the first transmission interval is based at least in part on determining the discovery response is not received. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising: 

 determining the second device is out of a signaling range of the first device. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein transmitting the first discovery request is based at least in part on determining the second device is out of the signaling range of the first device. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the first device and the second device are mobile stations (STAs). 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the first device and the second device operate within a wireless local area network (WLAN). 12. An apparatus for wireless communication, comprising: a communications manager for establishing, at a first device, a device-to- device wireless connection with a second device; a transmitter for transmitting a first discovery request at a first transmission interval; wherein the communications manager further determines whether a discovery response to the first discovery request is received from the second device; and an interval adjuster for adjusting the first transmission interval to a second transmission interval based at least in part on the determination. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the device-to-device wireless connection comprises a tunneled direct link setup (TDLS) connection. 14. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: an interval comparer for comparing the second transmission interval with a maximum transmission interval. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the interval comparer further determines the second transmission interval is less than the maximum transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison; and wherein the transmitter further transmits a second discovery request at the second transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison. 16. The apparatus of claim 14, further comprising: 

 the interval comparer for determining the second transmission interval is greater than the maximum transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison; and the transmitter for transmitting a second discovery request at the maximum transmission interval. 17. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the second transmission interval is a multiple of the first transmission interval. 18. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the interval adjuster further adjusts the first transmission interval is based at least in part on determining the discovery response is not received. 19. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: an interval comparer for determining the second device is out of a signaling range of the first device. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein the transmitter further transmits the first discovery request is based at least in part on determining the second device is out of the signaling range of the first device. 21. An apparatus for wireless communication, comprising: a processor; memory in electronic communication with the processor; and instructions stored in the memory and operable, when executed by the processor, to cause the apparatus to: establish, at a first device, a device-to-device wireless connection with a second device; transmit a first discovery request at a first transmission interval; determine whether a discovery response to the first discovery request is received from the second device; and adjust the first transmission interval to a second transmission interval based at least in part on the determination. 22. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the device-to-device wireless connection comprises a tunneled direct link setup (TDLS) connection. 23. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the instructions are operable to cause the processor to: compare the second transmission interval with a maximum transmission interval. 24. The apparatus of claim 23, wherein the instructions are operable to cause the processor to: determine the second transmission interval is less than the maximum transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison; and transmit a second discovery request at the second transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison. 25. The apparatus of claim 23, wherein the instructions are operable to cause the processor to: determine the second transmission interval is greater than the maximum transmission interval based at least in part on the comparison; and transmit a second discovery request at the maximum transmission interval. 26. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the second transmission interval is a multiple of the first transmission interval. 27. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the instructions to adjust the first transmission interval comprise instructions operable to adjust the first transmission interval based at least in part on determining the discovery response is not received. 28. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the instructions are operable to cause the processor to: determine the second device is out of a signaling range of the first device. 29. A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing code for wireless communication, the code comprising instructions executable to: establish, at a first device, a device-to-device wireless connection with a second device; transmit a first discovery request at a first transmission interval; 

 determine whether a discovery response to the first discovery request is received from the second device; and adjust the first transmission interval to a second transmission interval based at least in part on the determination. 30. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 29, wherein the device-to-device wireless connection comprises a tunneled direct link setup (TDLS) connection.
